# Master Report Template Structure

**Source Document:** VAL251012_-_North_Battleford_Apt.docx  
**Document Type:** Appraisal Report - Multifamily Property  
**Total Pages (Est.):** 44  
**Report Date:** January 2025

---

## 1. PAGE-BY-PAGE BREAKDOWN

| Footer Pg | Section Header | Subsection | Page Title | Content Type | Notes |
|-----------|---|---|---|---|---|
| — | (none) | (none) | Cover | cover | Title page with property & appraiser info |
| — | (none) | (none) | Transmittal Letter | letter | Client letter, signed by appraiser |
| — | (none) | (none) | Table of Contents | toc | Auto-generated or manual TOC |
| 1 | Introduction & Executive Summary | Property Overview | Property Overview (HBU/Value Summary) | table | Key property stats, value conclusion, HBU |
| 2 | Introduction & Executive Summary | Photographs | Photographs | images | 3-4 exterior property photos |
| 4–5 | Introduction & Executive Summary | Identification of Assignment | Property Details & Identification | narrative | Assignment identification, legal description, scope of work |
| 6 | Property Analysis | Location | Location Analysis | narrative | Regional/neighborhood context, access, infrastructure |
| 7–8 | Property Analysis | Site Details | Site Details & Physical Characteristics | narrative | Soil, utilities, easements, environmental, site rating |
| 9 | Property Analysis | Site Details | Site Plans – Lot 17 & 18 | full-page-image | Survey/site plan drawings |
| 10 | Property Analysis | Property Taxes & Assessment | Real Estate Taxes | table | Current assessment, tax rates, tax analysis |
| 11 | Property Analysis | Land Use & Planning | Zoning & Land Use Analysis | narrative | Current zoning, permitted uses, zoning compliance |
| 12 | Property Analysis | Land Use & Planning | Zoning Map | full-page-image | Municipal zoning map with property highlighted |
| 13 | Property Analysis | Description of the Improvements | Building Description & Overview | narrative | Building age, construction, unit mix, amenities, condition |
| 16 | Market Context | Economic Overviews | National & Provincial Economic Overview | narrative | Macro economic context, employment, GDP |
| 17 | Market Context | Market Overview | Saskatchewan Market Analysis | narrative | Provincial market trends, vacancy, rent growth |
| 18–20 | Market Context | Highest & Best Use | Highest & Best Use Analysis | narrative | LPPF analysis, HBU conclusion |
| 21 | Market Context | Valuation Methodology | Valuation Approaches Overview | narrative | Overview of three approaches used |
| 22 | Income Approach | Revenue Analysis | Subject Rent Roll & Market Rent Survey | table | Current leases, market rent comparables |
| 23–25 | Income Approach | Revenue Analysis | Rent Comparables Analysis | narrative | Market rent analysis, lease comparison, rent conclusion |
| 26 | Income Approach | Operating Expenses | Operating Expense Analysis | table | Operating expenses breakdown, $/unit/year |
| 27–28 | Income Approach | Capitalization Rate Selection | Cap Rate Analysis & Market Data | narrative | Market cap rates, investor survey, cap rate selection |
| 29–30 | Income Approach | Direct Capitalization Conclusion | Income Approach Value Conclusion | narrative | NOI calculation, cap rate application, value conclusion |
| 31 | Sales Comparison Approach | Comparable Selection | Sales Comparable Selection & Analysis | table | Comparable properties grid, metrics |
| 32–35 | Sales Comparison Approach | Analysis of Comparable Sales | Comparable Sales Summary | narrative | Individual comp analysis, adjustments, reconciliation |
| 36–37 | Sales Comparison Approach | Sales Comparison Approach Conclusion | SCA Reconciliation & Conclusion | narrative | Comparable analysis summary, value indication |
| 38 | Reconciliation of Value | (none) | Reconciliation of Three Approaches | narrative | Reconciliation matrix, final value conclusion, reasoning |
| 39–40 | Certification | (none) | Appraiser Certification & Signature Block | form | USPAP certification, signature, credentials, date |
| 41 | Appendices | Contingent & Limiting Conditions | Assumptions, Limiting Conditions, Disclaimers | appendix | Legal disclaimers, assumptions, limiting conditions |
| 42–43 | Appendices | Definition of Terms | Definition of Terms & Terminology | appendix | Appraisal terminology definitions |
| 44 | Appendices | Qualifications of the Appraiser | Appraiser Qualifications | form | Appraiser CV, designations, experience, licenses |

---

## 2. SECTION HIERARCHY TREE

```
APPRAISAL REPORT
│
├─ PRELIMINARY PAGES (unnumbered)
│  ├─ Cover
│  ├─ Transmittal Letter
│  └─ Table of Contents
│
├─ INTRODUCTION & EXECUTIVE SUMMARY (Pages 1–5)
│  ├─ Property Overview
│  │  └─ [HBU/Value Conclusion Table]
│  │  └─ [Hypothetical Conditions]
│  │  └─ [Extraordinary Assumptions]
│  │  └─ [Extraordinary Limiting Conditions]
│  ├─ Photographs
│  │  └─ [4 exterior photos]
│  └─ Identification of Assignment
│     ├─ Property Identification
│     ├─ Legal Description
│     ├─ Authorized Client Identification
│     ├─ Authorized Use & Authorized Users
│     ├─ Effective Date of Value and Report Date
│     ├─ Inspection Date
│     ├─ Purpose
│     ├─ Hypothetical Conditions
│     ├─ Extraordinary Assumptions
│     ├─ Extraordinary Limiting Conditions
│     ├─ Property And Sales History
│     │  ├─ Current Owner
│     │  └─ Three-Year Sales History
│     ├─ Exposure & Marketing Time
│     ├─ Definition of Market Value
│     ├─ Property Rights Appraised
│     │  └─ Fee Simple Interest
│     ├─ Value Scenarios
│     │  └─ Current Value
│     ├─ Scope of Work
│     ├─ Assistance Provided
│     ├─ Sources of Information
│     ├─ Subject Property Inspection
│     └─ Personal Property & Business Intangible
│
├─ PROPERTY ANALYSIS (Pages 6–15)
│  ├─ Location
│  │  └─ [Regional/neighborhood narrative]
│  ├─ Site Details
│  │  ├─ Exposure & Visibility
│  │  ├─ Easements
│  │  ├─ Soils
│  │  ├─ Hazardous Waste
│  │  ├─ Site Rating
│  │  └─ Site Conclusion
│  ├─ Site Plans – Lot 17
│  ├─ Site Plans – Lot 18
│  ├─ Property Taxes & Assessment
│  │  ├─ Real Estate Taxes
│  │  └─ Taxation & Assessment Commentary
│  ├─ Land Use & Planning
│  │  ├─ Zoning Conclusion
│  │  └─ Zoning Map
│  └─ Description of the Improvements
│     ├─ Overview
│     └─ Building Description
│
├─ MARKET CONTEXT (Pages 16–21)
│  ├─ Economic Overviews
│  │  ├─ National
│  │  ├─ Provincial
│  │  └─ Saskatchewan
│  ├─ Market Overview
│  │  └─ Saskatchewan
│  ├─ Highest & Best Use
│  │  ├─ Introduction
│  │  ├─ Analysis
│  │  │  ├─ Legally Permissible
│  │  │  ├─ Physically Possible
│  │  │  ├─ Financial Feasibility
│  │  │  └─ Maximum Productivity
│  │  ├─ [Re]Analysis
│  │  └─ Most Probable Buyer
│  └─ Valuation Methodology
│     ├─ Land Valuation
│     ├─ Cost Approach
│     ├─ Sales Comparison Approach
│     ├─ Income Capitalization Approach
│     └─ Correlation and Conclusion
│
├─ INCOME APPROACH (Pages 22–30)
│  ├─ Direct Capitalization Method
│  ├─ Revenue Analysis
│  │  ├─ Subject Rent Roll
│  │  ├─ Market Rent Survey Analysis
│  │  ├─ Unit of Comparison
│  │  ├─ Selection of Comparables
│  │  ├─ Concessions
│  │  ├─ Presentation
│  │  ├─ Conclusion Of Market Rent
│  │  ├─ Unit Rent Discussion
│  │  └─ Contract Versus Market Rent
│  ├─ Total Rental Revenue
│  ├─ Potential Gross Revenue (PGR)
│  ├─ Vacancy Allowance
│  ├─ Effective Gross Revenue (EGR)
│  ├─ Operating Expenses
│  ├─ Expense Conclusions
│  ├─ Net Operating Income (NOI)
│  ├─ Capitalization Rate Selection
│  │  ├─ [Rate data]
│  │  ├─ Alternative Investment Rates
│  │  ├─ Investment Activity and Trends
│  │  ├─ Capitalization Rate
│  │  ├─ Capitalization Rate Analysis – Multifamily
│  │  └─ Capitalization Rate Conclusion
│  ├─ Direct Capitalization Conclusion
│  └─ Income Approach Conclusion
│
├─ SALES COMPARISON APPROACH (Pages 31–37)
│  ├─ Introduction
│  ├─ Comparable Selection
│  ├─ Unit of Comparison
│  ├─ Adjustments
│  ├─ Quantitative Adjustment Process
│  ├─ Presentation
│  ├─ Sales Summary Sheets
│  ├─ Analysis of Comparable Sales
│  │  └─ [5 Comparable properties with detailed analysis]
│  └─ Sales Comparison Approach Conclusion
│
├─ RECONCILIATION OF VALUE (Pages 38)
│  ├─ Hypothetical Conditions
│  ├─ Extraordinary Assumptions
│  ├─ Extraordinary Limiting Conditions
│  ├─ [Reconciliation matrix]
│  └─ Final Estimate of Value
│
├─ CERTIFICATION (Pages 39–40)
│  ├─ Final Estimate of Value
│  ├─ Hypothetical Conditions
│  ├─ Extraordinary Assumptions
│  ├─ Extraordinary Limiting Conditions
│  └─ [Appraiser Signature Block & Credentials]
│
└─ APPENDICES (Pages 41–44)
   ├─ Contingent & Limiting Conditions
   │  └─ Assumptions, Limiting Conditions, Disclaimers and Limitations of Liability
   ├─ Definition of Terms
   │  └─ [Appraisal terminology glossary]
   └─ Qualifications of the Appraiser
      └─ [Appraiser CV and credentials]
```

---

## 3. CONTENT TYPE PATTERNS

### By Category

**Cover & Front Matter (Unnumbered)**
- Content Type: `cover`, `letter`, `toc`
- Header Style: None (title pages)
- Special Notes: No footer page numbers

**Data Tables (≈7 pages)**
- Content Type: `table`
- Pages: 1, 10, 22, 26, 31
- Characteristics: Organized data with multiple rows/columns, bold headers
- Header Style: Header2 for section, Subheader1 for subsection
- Field Types: Property stats, rent rolls, taxes, cap rates, comps

**Images & Maps (≈4 pages)**
- Content Type: `images` (photo grid) or `full-page-image` (plans/maps)
- Pages: 2, 9, 12
- Characteristics: Multi-photo grids, survey plans, zoning maps
- Header Style: Header2 for section
- Size: Full-page or 2-3 images per page

**Narrative Text (≈25 pages)**
- Content Type: `narrative`
- Pages: 4–8, 11, 13, 16–21, 23–25, 27–30, 32–38
- Characteristics: Body text with subheadings, lists, analysis paragraphs
- Header Style: Header1 (section), Header2 (subsection), Subheader1 (detail)
- Typical structure: Intro paragraph → bullet list → analysis → conclusion

**Forms & Signature Blocks (≈3 pages)**
- Content Type: `form`
- Pages: 39–40, 44
- Characteristics: Structured fields, signature lines, credentials
- Header Style: Header1 for "Certification"
- Special: Certification language, dates, licenses

**Boilerplate/Appendix Text (≈3 pages)**
- Content Type: `appendix`
- Pages: 41–43
- Characteristics: Legal disclaimers, standard definitions, long-form text
- Header Style: Header1 for "Appendices", Header2 for subsection
- Content: Static across reports (rarely changes)

### By Pattern Frequency

| Pattern | Frequency | Pages | Example |
|---------|-----------|-------|---------|
| Narrative + Subheader | High (15×) | 4–8, 11, 13–20, 23–38 | "Identification of Assignment" |
| Table with narrative | Medium (5×) | 1, 10, 22, 26, 31 | "Property Overview", "Rent Roll" |
| Full-page image | Low (3×) | 2, 9, 12 | Site plans, zoning map, photos |
| Form/signature | Low (2×) | 39–40, 44 | Certification, appraiser bio |
| Pure appendix | Low (2×) | 41–43 | Limiting conditions, definitions |

---

## 4. HEADER & STYLE CONSISTENCY NOTES

### Header Levels & Styling

**Header 1 (Blue Bar Section Header)**
- Appears at top of each major section
- Sections: Introduction & Executive Summary, Property Analysis, Market Context, Income Approach, Sales Comparison Approach, Reconciliation of Value, Certification, Appendices
- Styling: Full-width blue bar (#003B7E), white text, uppercase
- Pattern: Used 8 times in document

**Header 2 (Left-Aligned Subsection Header)**
- Appears below Header 1 to group related content
- Examples: Property Overview, Photographs, Location, Site Details, Economic Overviews
- Styling: Left-aligned, bold, blue text (#003B7E) or black with blue underline
- Pattern: Used 16 times in document
- Special Note: Style is "Header 2 -" (with dash) in Word

**Subheader 1 (Detail Level)**
- Appears within sections to break up content
- Examples: "Property Identification", "Legal Description", "Current Owner"
- Styling: Bold, left-aligned, black text
- Pattern: Used extensively (40+ times)

**Subheader 2 (Minor Detail Level)**
- Used for sub-topics within subsections
- Examples: "Fee Simple Interest", "Current Value", "Saskatchewan"
- Styling: Bold, left-aligned, slightly smaller than Subheader1
- Pattern: Used occasionally (10+ times)

### Consistency Observations

✓ **Consistent:**
- Header 1 always precedes Header 2
- Header 2 always introduces distinct subsection
- Subheader 1 used for narrative breakpoints
- Color scheme consistent (blue #003B7E for headers)

⚠️ **Variations to Note:**
- Some Subheader 1 entries are empty placeholders (appears 3× in doc)
- "Header 2 -" has trailing dash in style name (vs. "Header 1")
- Market Context section has some nested Subheader2 within analysis

⚠️ **Recommendations for Template:**
1. Always include Header 1 at section start
2. Always include Header 2 for subsections (never skip)
3. Subheader 1 for logical breaks in narrative (3-5 per page typical)
4. Use Subheader 2 rarely—only for specific nested topics (property rights, scenarios)

---

## 5. FIELD MAPPING CANDIDATES

### High-Frequency Dynamic Fields (appear on multiple pages)

```
{{Property_Address}}
{{File_Number}}
{{Property_City_Province}}
{{Effective_Date_Value}}
{{Report_Date}}
{{Appraiser_Name_Credentials}}
{{Final_Value_Conclusion}}
```

### Section-Specific Dynamic Fields

**Introduction & Executive Summary**
```
{{Property_Overview_Table}} [Property stats, HBU, value conclusion]
{{Current_Owner_Name}}
{{Three_Year_Sale_History}} [Table]
{{Inspection_Date}}
{{Authorized_Client}}
```

**Property Analysis**
```
{{Property_Tax_Current}}
{{Property_Assessment_Value}}
{{Tax_Rate_Percent}}
{{Zoning_Designation}}
{{Building_Condition_Summary}}
{{Year_Built}}
{{Total_Units}}
```

**Income Approach**
```
{{Market_Rent_Per_Unit}}
{{Vacancy_Rate_Percent}}
{{Operating_Expenses_Per_Unit}}
{{NOI_Stabilized}}
{{Cap_Rate_Applied_Percent}}
{{Income_Indicated_Value}}
{{COMP1_TransactionPrice}}, {{COMP2_...}}, etc.
{{COMP_AVG_Price_Per_Unit}}
```

**Sales Comparison Approach**
```
{{COMP1_Address}}, {{COMP2_Address}}, ... [5 comps]
{{COMP1_SalePrice}}, {{COMP2_SalePrice}}, ...
{{COMP1_PricePerUnit}}, {{COMP2_PricePerUnit}}, ...
{{SCA_IndicatedValue}}
```

| Metric | Value | Notes |

| Total Images | 28 | Photos, site plans, maps, charts |
| Major Sections | 8 | From cover to appendices |
| Subsections | 20+ | Nested under Header 2 |

| Color Usage | #003B7E, #0072CE | Brand blue for headers and accents |

---

## 7. TEMPLATE USAGE GUIDE

### When Converting a Page

1. **Identify Footer Page Number** → Look up in column 1 of table
2. **Get Section Header** → Column 2 (use Header1 style)
3. **Get Subsection** → Column 3 (use Header2 style)
4. **Match Content Type** → Column 5 (narrative, table, image, etc.)
5. **Extract Fields** → Look up dynamic fields in section 5
6. **Add Footer** → All pages include: `{{Property_Address}} | File {{File_Number}}`

### Quick Reference by Content Type

**narrative** (most common)
```html
<div class="Header1">Section Name</div>
<div class="Header2">Subsection Name</div>
<p>Paragraph text...</p>
<div class="Subheader1">Detail Break</div>
<p>More content...</p>
```

**table** (data pages)
```html
<div class="Header2">Section Name</div>
<table>
  <thead>
    <tr><th>Column 1</th><th>Column 2</th></tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>...</tbody>
</table>
```

**form** (signature pages)
```html
<div class="Header1">Certification</div>
<p>Certification language...</p>
<div class="signature-block">
  Signature: ________________
  Name: {{Appraiser_Name_Credentials}}
</div>
```

**full-page-image** (maps, plans)
```html
<div class="Header2">Section Name</div>
<img src="[base64-or-path]" class="full-page-image">
```

---

## 8. NEXT STEPS

To use this template:

1. **For Page 1**: Use header "Introduction & Executive Summary", subheader "Property Overview", create data table with property stats
2. **For Page 2**: Use same header, subheader "Photographs", create 3-4 image grid
3. **For Page 4+**: Continue down table, matching headers and content types

All pages should include footer with `{{Property_Address}}` and `{{File_Number}}`.
